parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
policy_statement: Measure ES1: Develop a sunset strategy for all oil and gas operations, including collaboration with jurisdictions/utilities and development of an ordinance.

policy_statement: Develop a policy requiring examination of all active, idle, and abandoned oil wells for fugitive GHG emissions and coordinate with agencies conducting fugitive emissions data.

policy_statement: Transition all County facilities within unincorporated areas to CPA's 100% Green Power option, SCE's 100% Green Rate option, or other available 100% renewable electricity service.

policy_statement: Measure ES3: Increase renewable energy production through requirements for rooftop solar in new development, solar installations on existing buildings and County facilities, community-shared solar, affordable housing renewable energy requirements/incentives, and streamlined permitting for solar and storage.

policy_statement: Measure ES4: Increase energy resilience by investing in storage and microgrids, deploying resilience hubs, creating an energy map, conducting feasibility studies for solar/storage microgrids and adopting regulations/standards, and promoting efficiency/resilience in critical service facilities.

policy_statement: Invest in energy storage and microgrids at critical County facilities through CPA's Power Ready Program.

policy_statement: Develop a publicly accessible community energy map identifying opportunities for distributed energy resources and microgrids to improve energy resiliency.

policy_statement: Develop a Countywide program to promote energy efficiency and resilience measures in facilities providing critical community services.

policy_statement: Measure ES5: Establish GHG requirements for new development through new requirements (reach codes/ordinances/conditions), a CAP consistency checklist, evaluating programs requiring General Plan amendments, and establishing an Offsite GHG Reduction Program.

parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
policy_statement: Identify new requirements for new development (reach codes, ordinances, conditions of approval) to reduce GHG emissions across energy, transportation, waste, water, and other sources, including affordability considerations and supporting measures to defray first costs.

policy_statement: Implement the 2045 CAP consistency review checklist for new development to demonstrate consistency with CAP strategies, measures, and actions.

parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
policy_statement: Evaluate a program for reducing GHG emissions for new development that require General Plan amendments.

parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
policy_statement: Establish an Offsite GHG Reduction Program for new development to use as a GHG reduction/mitigation pathway for CAP compliance and to fund programs reducing GHG emissions in the built environment.

parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
policy_statement: Measure E2: Standardize all-electric new development by adopting an ordinance requiring applicable new buildings be fully electric (no natural gas hookups), adopting ZNE ordinances by specified years, and adopting CALGreen Tier 1 standards and potential Tier 2 amendments.

parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
policy_statement: Adopt an ordinance requiring all applicable new buildings to be fully electric with no natural gas hookups, with affordability considerations and supporting measures to defray first costs.

parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
policy_statement: Adopt a ZNE ordinance for all new residential buildings built after 2025 and all new nonresidential buildings built after 2030, including renter protections and affordable housing set-aside to offset first cost.

parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
policy_statement: Adopt CALGreen Code Tier 1 green building standards and identify Tier 2 standards for potential adoption as code amendments.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
policy_statement: Measure T1 will increase density near High-Quality Transit Areas (HQTAs) by setting minimum and maximum dwelling unit density ranges, locating most residential and employment centers within 1 mile of an HQTA, and increasing dwelling units within HQTAs.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
policy_statement: Measure T2 will develop land use plans to address jobs-housing balance and increase mixed use, including achieving job density targets for new nonresidential projects and requiring community plans to identify strategies to reduce jobs/housing imbalance below 20%.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
policy_statement: Measure T3 will expand the bicycle and pedestrian network, including increasing bikeway miles by 300% by 2035 and implementing the County Bicycle Master Plan and updating pedestrian/bicycle/active transportation plans every five years.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
policy_statement: Measure T4 will broaden options for transit and alternative modes by 2030 by doubling transit service hours, installing bus-only lanes and signal prioritization on major transit thoroughfares, and ensuring at least 75% of unincorporated County residents live within one-half mile of a bus or active transportation option.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
policy_statement: Measure T5 will limit and remove parking minimums through parking supply reductions, unbundling parking costs, and implementing parking pricing to encourage park-once behavior.

parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
policy_statement: Measure T6 will increase zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) market share and EV charging stations, including targets for fleetwide ZEV penetration, ZEV sales shares, total new EV charging stations, and EV charging stations at County facilities by 2030/2035/2045.

parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
policy_statement: Measure T7 will electrify County fleet vehicles by electrifying bus and shuttle fleets by 2035, increasing County-owned light-duty ZEV shares by 2030/2035/2045, and supporting the state's goal that new light-duty fleet purchases (with exceptions) will be ZEVs.

parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
policy_statement: Measure T8 will accelerate freight decarbonization with ZEV targets for medium- and heavy-duty fleets and drayage, ZEV sales targets, and requirements for EV charging stations at new and existing warehouse loading docks by 2030.

parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
policy_statement: Measure T9 will expand use of zero-emission off-road vehicles and equipment by increasing ZEV shares for off-road fleets and for construction/agriculture/manufacturing equipment by 2030/2035/2045.
